_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase over that period. This represents a significant rise over the past several years, reflecting recent and uneven price changes across the different components. The index aggregates these soft commodities to provide a simplified view of breakfast-related food costs globally. However, not all items contributed equally to this headline higher reading.
notes.movers: The primary drivers of the increase are coffee, up 93% since 2015, and orange juice, up 22%, while wheat and sugar have seen modest rises of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price moves are largely the result of weather events and crop diseases rather than broad inflationary pressures. For example, frost and drought have impacted arabica coffee regions, while citrus greening and hurricanes have affected orange juice production. These soft commodities trade in thin markets, meaning small shifts in supply can cause pronounced price volatility.
notes.why: This price dynamic underscores how a few weather-sensitive and disease-exposed crops can disproportionately influence overall commodity indexes. Given the nature of these risks, input prices for such soft commodities can remain elevated for extended periods, reflecting ongoing supply vulnerabilities and market sensitivities.
